title: arceuthobium nigrum
intro: Arceuthobium nigrum, also known as the black dwarf mistletoe or black gall mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that grows on the branches of pine and spruce trees. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Arceuthobium nigrum are also attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Arceuthobium nigrum can be prepared for herbal use by drying the plant material, then making teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
